strengths: Proven distributed-systems depth: production microservices handling millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, event-driven architecture, low-latency async I/O work in D
Early but relevant agentic-AI exposure: current role at re:cinq includes MCP code generators, agentic workflow frameworks, and AI-agent infrastructure contributions
Meaningful fintech adjacency: hands-on work at RIDE Capital (investment platform) and Optilyz (pricing engine across countries), demonstrating comfort in commercially consequential software
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership at scale: entire career is individual-contributor and staff-engineer roles with no evidence of managing, growing, or structuring engineering teams of any size
Agentic AI experience is tooling-level and very recent (13 months), not production multi-agent systems with real-time inference, feedback loops, and regulatory-grade auditability as the JD requires

No executive or board-facing experience: the role demands roadmap communication to leadership and investors, governance of outsourced partners, and org design — none of which appear anywhere in the resume
response.justification: Bogdan is a technically strong individual contributor with genuine distributed-systems credentials and early exposure to agentic tooling, but the CTO role demands a profile that is fundamentally different from what the resume presents: a proven engineering executive who has scaled teams, owned architectural governance, operated production AI in regulated fintech environments, and engaged at board level. The career trajectory shows depth and craft, but no inflection point toward people leadership, org design, or the executive accountability the role requires. The agentic AI work, while directionally relevant, is too nascent and tooling-focused to anchor the multi-agent architecture ownership central to this position.
